verses.text: 'Better is a dry morsel, and quietness therewith, than an house full of sacrifices with strife'; a wise servant shall rule a shameful son; 'the fining pot is for silver... but the LORD trieth the hearts.' Peace beats plenty. God tests the heart as fire tests metal.

'Better is a dry morsel, and quietness therewith, than an house full of sacrifices with strife'; a wise servant shall rule a shameful son; 'the fining pot is for silver... but the LORD trieth the hearts.' Peace beats plenty. God tests the heart as fire tests metal.

'Better is a dry morsel, and quietness therewith, than an house full of sacrifices with strife'; a wise servant shall rule a shameful son; 'the fining pot is for silver... but the LORD trieth the hearts.' Peace beats plenty. God tests the heart as fire tests metal.

A wicked doer heeds false lips, and a liar gives ear to a naughty tongue; he that mocks the poor reproaches his Maker; 'children's children are the crown of old men, and the glory of children are their fathers.' Mockery insults God. Family is a shared crown of honour.

A wicked doer heeds false lips, and a liar gives ear to a naughty tongue; he that mocks the poor reproaches his Maker; 'children's children are the crown of old men, and the glory of children are their fathers.' Mockery insults God. Family is a shared crown of honour.

A wicked doer heeds false lips, and a liar gives ear to a naughty tongue; he that mocks the poor reproaches his Maker; 'children's children are the crown of old men, and the glory of children are their fathers.' Mockery insults God. Family is a shared crown of honour.

Excellent speech suits not a fool, nor lying a prince; 'he that covereth a transgression seeketh love; but he that repeateth a matter separateth very friends.' Love conceals faults. To forgive and hush a wrong keeps friendship; to broadcast it divides.

Excellent speech suits not a fool, nor lying a prince; 'he that covereth a transgression seeketh love; but he that repeateth a matter separateth very friends.' Love conceals faults. To forgive and hush a wrong keeps friendship; to broadcast it divides.

A reproof enters more into a wise man than a hundred stripes into a fool; an evil man seeks only rebellion; better to meet a robbed bear than a fool in his folly. The wise heed a word. But a fool is past correcting and more dangerous than a beast.

Whoso rewards evil for good, evil shall not depart from his house; 'the beginning of strife is as when one letteth out water,' so leave off before it bursts; 'he that justifieth the wicked... are abomination to the LORD.' Stop strife early. God hates the perverting of justice.

Why should a fool buy wisdom when he has no heart for it? a true friend loves at all times, and a brother is born for adversity; a man void of understanding strikes hands in surety. Friendship is proved in trouble. Wisdom is wasted on the heart that does not want it.

He that loves strife loves transgression, and the proud-gated invites destruction; the froward heart finds no good; 'he that begetteth a fool doeth it to his sorrow.' Pride courts ruin. A perverse tongue and heart bring only trouble and grief.

He that loves strife loves transgression, and the proud-gated invites destruction; the froward heart finds no good; 'he that begetteth a fool doeth it to his sorrow.' Pride courts ruin. A perverse tongue and heart bring only trouble and grief.

'A merry heart doeth good like a medicine: but a broken spirit drieth the bones'; the wicked takes a bribe to pervert judgment; 'wisdom is before him that hath understanding,' but a fool's eyes wander to the ends of the earth. Gladness heals. The wise keep wisdom in view, while fools are distracted.

Gladness heals. The wise keep wisdom in view, while fools are distracted.

'A merry heart doeth good like a medicine: but a broken spirit drieth the bones'; the wicked takes a bribe to pervert judgment; 'wisdom is before him that hath understanding,' but a fool's eyes wander to the ends of the earth. Gladness heals. The wise keep wisdom in view, while fools are distracted.

A foolish son grieves his parents; to punish the just is not good; he that has knowledge spares his words, and 'even a fool, when he holdeth his peace, is counted wise.' Restraint is wisdom. The wise speak little, and silence can hide even a fool's folly.
